findings.title: generateText and generateStructured share identical routing logic
findings.description: Both generateText (lines 135-144) and generateStructured (lines 146-159) perform the same 4-step routing sequence: classifyTask -> resolveTier -> getProvider -> increment stats. The only differences are (1) classifyTask receives an optional schemaDescription in generateStructured, and (2) they delegate to different provider methods. This is a 6-line near-duplicate block.

findings.title: Test setup (createTrackingProvider + new ModelRouter) repeated 12 times
findings.description: The 4-line pattern of creating lightweight + standard tracking providers and a ModelRouter appears in 10 of 14 test cases (lines 71-75, 88-92, 105-109, 121-125, 174-179, 194-199, 213-216, 235-239, 255-259, 293-296). This is well above the 3-occurrence threshold for extraction. The 3-provider variant (with local) appears in 3 more tests.

findings.title: Magic number 5 in areSimilar default threshold
findings.description: The default threshold of 5 in areSimilar is a magic number. It represents 'maximum Hamming distance for similarity on a 64-bit hash' but is not given a named constant. The codebase already defines HASH_SIZE = 8 as a named constant in the same file; threshold deserves similar treatment.

findings.title: Image pixel generation loops repeated 3 times in dedup-stats test
findings.description: The beforeAll block generates 3 test images using nearly identical nested loops (lines 109-148). Each loop iterates pixels and sets RGB values with only the color values differing. Per CLAUDE.md clarity principles, three similar lines are acceptable over premature abstraction, but here it is 3 blocks of 8+ lines each (~24 lines of near-identical structure). A helper would reduce this to ~12 lines total.

positive_observations: classifyTask is a well-structured pure function with clear keyword-based classification and a safe default fallback to 'generation'
ModelRouter follows the project's Functional Core / Imperative Shell pattern: pure classifyTask + resolveTier functions with a stateful class shell
DeduplicationTracker is impressively simple (3 methods, 2 counters) with correct edge-case handling for zero comparisons
Named constants HASH_SIZE, CLASSIFICATION_KEYWORDS etc. follow project convention for avoiding magic values
All functions are well under 30 lines, matching the project's established complexity thresholds
Test Double rationale comments are present in all test files, following project convention
